commit.message:
User/yashasg/beatmap improvement (#19)

* fix: derive rhythm positions from song_time, not accumulated dt

Applied the key insight from fizzd's LD48 rhythm game postmortem:
'Every object that needs to be in sync should do so using only the
song position, and NOT anything else.'

scroll_system: rhythm obstacles (with BeatInfo) now compute position
as SPAWN_Y + (song_time - spawn_time) * scroll_speed instead of
accumulating pos.y += vel.dy * dt each frame. This eliminates
floating-point drift that causes obstacles to slowly desync from the
beat grid over time. Non-rhythm entities (particles, popups, freeplay
obstacles) still use dt-based movement.

shape_window_system: window_timer is now derived from
song_time - window_start instead of accumulated dt. Phase transitions
increment window_start by the exact phase duration (not the current
song_time), following the 'never update your reference point
arbitrarily — only increment by set amounts' rule.

Tests updated to advance song_time instead of relying on dt passthrough.

All 383 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use RenderTexture letterbox in Emscripten path to prevent squish

The Emscripten main loop was rendering directly to the screen canvas
(BeginDrawing → render_system → EndDrawing) while the native path
rendered to a 720×1280 RenderTexture then blitted it letterboxed.
This caused the game to stretch/squish to whatever size the browser
canvas was.

Now both paths use the same RenderTexture → DrawTexturePro letterbox
pipeline. The LoopState struct carries the RenderTexture handle so
the Emscripten callback can access it.

Also simplified CSS: removed media queries in favor of a single rule
(max-width:100vw; max-height:100vh; object-fit:contain) that works
for both mobile and desktop since the letterboxing is handled in C++.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: rewrite level_designer with music-driven obstacle assignment

Rewrote level_designer.py based on research from 5argon's notecharting
guide, DDRKirby's Rhythm Quest devlog, and Liang et al.'s PCG paper.

Architecture changed from combo-rotation to 3-phase pipeline:
  Phase 1 (SELECT): density from song structure + flux threshold.
    Repeated section types reuse the same relative beat pattern
    so players experience 'second time = mastery'.
  Phase 2 (ASSIGN): onset passes determine obstacle type directly.
    kick-only → high_bar, hihat-only → low_bar, snare → lane_block,
    multi-pass/melody → shape_gate. Variety injection forces non-shape
    obstacles after consecutive runs, preventing monotony.
  Phase 3 (CLEAN): safety net removes violations (unchanged rules).
    Added gap monotony cleaner: no single gap value > 40%.

Results vs old generator:
  Gap monotony: 20-35% (was 41-47%)
  Hard variety: 73% shape_gate + 20% lane + 6% bars (was 60/32/4)
  Pattern reuse: verse 1 and verse 2 share same beat pattern
  Section-aware: bridge=sparse, drop=dense+consistent, intro=minimal

Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json with new generator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add --difficulty CLI param (easy/medium/hard)

Usage:
  ./build/shapeshifter --difficulty easy
  ./build/shapeshifter --difficulty hard --test-player pro
  ./run.sh --difficulty easy

Defaults to 'medium' when not specified. The difficulty parameter
is passed to load_beat_map() which selects the matching difficulty
tier from the beatmap JSON.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: disable jump/slide — game focuses on shape + lane only

Removed jump (swipe up / W) and slide (swipe down / S) from active
gameplay. The game was too complex with 3 simultaneous mechanics
(shape matching + lane dodging + vertical movement).

Changes:
- player_action_system: removed jump/slide gesture handling
- level_designer: removed low_bar/high_bar from all difficulty tiers
  and section roles
- Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json (shape_gate + lane_block only)
- Updated tests to verify jump/slide are disabled

Engine code for VerticalState/VMode remains intact (dead code) so
the feature can be re-enabled later if desired.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: flow-based shape picker replaces frequency-based assignment

Shape selection now driven by playability flow, not onset pass names.
Based on 5argon/DDRKirby research: 'accuracy to the song is less
important than how fun it feels to play.'

New rules (all in Phase 2 generation, no overlap with Phase 3 cleanup):
1. Section palette: intro/bridge = 1 shape (center lane only),
   verse = 2 shapes (circle+square), pre-chorus/drop = all 3.
   This creates a natural difficulty ramp as shapes are introduced.
2. Short gaps (≤2 beats): repeat same shape (groove/L1-kick principle).
   Player stays locked in rhythm without disruption.
3. Long gaps (3+ beats): switch to different shape from palette.
   Player has time to prepare; variety feels earned.
4. Anti-triple: built into picker, never 3 of same in a row.

Removed dead code: PASS_TO_SHAPE dict, dominant_pass() function.

Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json. Intro is now square-only,
verses use 2 shapes, drops use all 3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase obstacle density and tile patterns across long sections

The beatmap felt too empty — hard mode had 0.27 obstacles/beat with
gaps up to 22 beats (9 seconds of nothing). Two causes:

1. Double-filtering: flux threshold removed 25% of events, then
   section density removed another 20-50%. Raised section densities
   (verse 0.50→0.65, drop 0.80→0.90) and difficulty scales
   (easy 0.50→0.55, medium 0.75→0.80). Lowered flux thresholds
   (hard 25→15 percentile).

2. Pattern stretching: when a 5.7s pre-chorus pattern was reused
   for a 29.7s section, it stretched across 5x the length leaving
   huge gaps. Now tiles the pattern to fill longer sections (e.g.
   29.7s section gets 5 repetitions of the 5.7s pattern).

Results:
  easy:   92→121 obs, 0.7 presses/sec, max gap 18 (intro)
  medium: 100→134 obs, 0.7 presses/sec, max gap 12
  hard:   123→153 obs, 0.8 presses/sec, max gap 13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: increase hard mode density to ~1 press/sec

Hard: scale 1.00→1.20, flux threshold p15→p5.
Now 171 obstacles at 0.9 presses/sec (was 153 at 0.8/sec).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: lower flux thresholds + skip CI on content/tools changes in PRs

Flux thresholds lowered: easy 70→50, medium 40→25. More musical
events survive filtering, giving denser levels:
  easy:   121→115 obs (slightly fewer due to event selection)
  medium: 134→150 obs (0.33 presses/sec)
  hard:   171 obs unchanged

CI: added paths-ignore to pull_request triggers in all 4 workflows.
Changes to content/, tools/, design-docs/, *.md, *.log no longer
trigger builds on PRs. Push to main still builds everything.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: preserve window shortening and late-spawn clamp under song-time timing

- collision_system: adjust window_start backward instead of advancing
  window_timer when scale < 1.0; shape_window_system re-derives
  window_timer from song_time - window_start every frame so the old
  mutation was silently discarded on the next tick.
- beat_scheduler_system: when late-spawn clamping fires, store an
  adjusted effective_spawn_time in BeatInfo so scroll_system
  reproduces the clamped start_y via its song-time formula.
- Update two existing window_scaling tests to assert window_start
  movement instead of window_timer advancement.
- Add four new tests: BAD/Perfect timing collision, clamped spawn
  time in BeatInfo.

* Implement pluggable Blazor auth with sign-in route

- Overhauled authentication UX: added `/signin` route, explicit route protection, and redirect for unauthorized access using Blazor's `AuthorizeRouteView`.
- Introduced pluggable auth abstraction (`IAuthService`, `AuthServiceFactory`, etc.) and a mock provider with hardcoded users.

* feat: Microsoft Entra ID integration with security hardening

Add live Microsoft Entra ID sign-in alongside the existing mock auth flow.
The config-driven AuthServiceFactory selects between mock, microsoft, or
combined mode without code changes.

Security hardening (Warden review):
- OIDC scheme registered conditionally — only when TenantId, ClientId,
  and ClientSecret are all present. Prevents unnecessary callback path
  exposure and metadata retrieval in mock-only mode.
- PKCE enabled (UsePkce = true) as defense-in-depth for auth code flow.
- Cookie hardened: explicit SecurePolicy=SameAsRequest, 8-hour sliding
  expiration, SlidingExpiration=true.
- OnTokenValidated uses context.Fail() instead of throwing, so the OIDC
  handler can return a proper error response rather than a 500.
- /auth/microsoft/signin endpoint only mapped when OIDC scheme exists,
  preventing information leakage from unregistered-scheme exceptions.
- /auth/signout universal endpoint clears the cookie first, then only
  attempts federated Entra sign-out when the OIDC scheme is registered.
- ClientSecret removed from committed appsettings.json — secrets live
  exclusively in dotnet user-secrets.
- MainLayout sign-out reverted to <a> tag (was <button @onclick>) so
  sign-out always works even when the Blazor SignalR circuit is degraded.

New files:
- MicrosoftEntraAuthService.cs — IAuthService for the live OIDC flow
- MicrosoftEntraAuthenticationOptions.cs — config with tenant seed maps
- CompositeAuthService.cs — merges mock + Microsoft providers
- AuthenticatedUserClaims.cs — shared claim read/write helpers

Existing demo regression: all 5 AuthServiceFactory + MockAuthService
unit tests pass unchanged with Authentication:Service=mock.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* security: gate mock auth endpoints behind service mode

Mock auth HTTP endpoints (/auth/mock/signin, /auth/mock/session, etc.)
were unconditionally registered in Program.cs. When Authentication:Service
was set to 'microsoft', these endpoints remained reachable, allowing
anyone to mint a valid session cookie as a mock user — bypassing
Microsoft Entra ID authentication entirely.

Fix: Mock endpoints are now only registered when the service mode is
not 'microsoft'. All other modes (mock, combined, auto) continue to
expose them as expected.

Also updated README with Azure app registration steps and explicit
service mode documentation.

Stop force-refreshing PRs with non-success CI or UNKNOWN mergeable (#77)

* Remove unconditional force-refresh for non-success CI and UNKNOWN mergeable

Previously, PRs with ci != SUCCESS or mergeable == UNKNOWN were always
re-fetched via GraphQL, even when the fingerprint was unchanged and the
entry was recently refreshed. This caused ~97% cache miss rates on
back-to-back runs of large repos like aspnetcore (173/177 refreshed).

CI reruns do not bump the PR updatedAt timestamp, so the fingerprint
alone cannot detect them. However, the existing 12-hour per-PR TTL
guarantees every entry is eventually refreshed, which is sufficient
for the dashboard's freshness promise.

Now these PRs follow the same fingerprint + TTL logic as all others:
reuse if fingerprint matches and _refreshed_at is within MaxReuseSec.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add complementary assertions to reuse/refresh tests

Each reuse test now also asserts RefreshCandidates.Count is 0, and each
refresh test asserts ReusedEntries.Count is 0, ensuring entries land in
exactly one partition.

commit.message:
fix: replace Unicode chars with ASCII; add UTF-8 BOM to post_install.ps1

PS 5.1 reads .ps1 files as ANSI (system codepage) when no BOM is present.
The em dash (U+2014, UTF-8: E2 80 94) was being misread: byte 0x94 maps to
U+201D (curly right-quote) in cp1252, which unbalances string delimiters and
causes 'Missing closing }' parse errors at runtime on the target machine.

Changes:
- Replace all U+2192 (arrow) with ASCII ->
- Replace all U+2014 (em dash) with ASCII --
- Write file with UTF-8 BOM so PS 5.1 reads it as UTF-8

commit.message:
Scale content on window resize (nearest-neighbor)

When the window is resized, the old pixel content is now scaled to fit
the new dimensions using nearest-neighbor sampling (integer math only).

- Enlarging: content scales up (pixelated zoom)
- Shrinking: content scales down (zoom out)
- Animated demos: unaffected (clear+redraw each frame)
- Static demos: show scaled version until next explicit redraw

Implemented in both Windows WM_SIZE and X11 ConfigureNotify handlers.

commit.message:
fix: validation uses argmax consistency instead of strict tolerance

Float16 TFLite quantization can exceed 1e-2 atol on certain models
while still predicting the correct class. The validator now uses a
tri-state result:
  pass — values within tolerance
  warn — values differ but predicted class (argmax) matches
  fail — predicted class differs (real problem)

Pipeline only fails if predicted classes actually differ. Float16
precision loss with matching argmax is logged as warning, not failure.

commit.message:
Adaptive sub-chunking for failed API queries (#76)

* Adaptive sub-chunking for failed API queries

When a 7-day chunk fails (e.g. GitHub 502), recursively halve into
smaller chunks (7->3->1 day) instead of giving up. Successful results
at any level are merged; only genuinely unrecoverable 1-day failures
are reported as FailedChunks.

- Add Merge-ScanResult helper to eliminate duplicated merge logic
- Add -MinChunkDays and -EndDate params to Invoke-ChunkedRepoScan
- Use actual-span guard to avoid redundant recursion on short tail chunks
- Replace count-based success check with anyChunkSucceeded boolean
- Add 4 new Pester tests for sub-chunking behavior

commit.message:
fix: detect WiX bundle entries in pre-clean; delete CPython-3.14 dependency key

Root cause of 'action: Modify' mode:
- Python 3.14.2 (64-bit) GUID {0ef3959c...} is a WiX Burn bundle ARP entry
- msiexec /x returns 1605 on bundles (not an MSI product) which was excluded
  from force-deletion, so the ARP key survived every pre-clean run
- Burn finds this ARP key on next launch -> enters Modify mode
- In Modify mode BA only requests core+exe, not lib_AllUsers -> no stdlib

Fix:
- Detect bundle entries by presence of BundleVersion registry value
- Skip msiexec for bundles; directly Remove-Item the ARP key
- After registry sweep, also delete the Burn dependency provider key
  HKLM/HKCU ..\Installer\Dependencies\CPython-3.14 which is the actual
  trigger for Burn's 'bundle is installed' detection

.expo/ and expo-env.d.ts are generated by the Expo CLI on every
start/build. They carry a 'do not edit' notice and must not be
reformatted by Biome.

- Register route (/register): redirect to / when already authenticated

* Require mobile API base URL

Fail fast in the mobile auth client when EXPO_PUBLIC_TODOS_API_URL is missing and align the documented default API port with the current local contract.

* Clean up MCP README boilerplate

Remove stale NestJS template reference lines and normalize the install snippet without mixing that cleanup into the mobile env-contract change.

* Pin Vitest versions across workspaces

Replace floating and divergent Vitest ranges with a single explicit workspace version so installs and lockfile refreshes stay deterministic.
